q: Which of the following is a dimensionless physical quantity?
exp: Strain is the ratio of change in length to original length, so it has no dimension.

q: The dimensional formula of angular momentum is:
exp: Angular momentum = moment of inertia × angular velocity = [ML^2][T^-1].

q: The SI unit of electric flux is:
exp: Electric flux = electric field × area, so unit = (N/C)m^2 = Nm^2/C.

q: Which pair has the same dimensions?
exp: Both pressure and stress have dimensional formula [ML^-1T^-2].

q: The number of significant figures in 0.005060 is:
exp: Leading zeros are not significant. Significant digits are 5,0,6,0 → 4 significant figures.

q: A quantity having dimensions [ML^-1T^-2] is:
exp: Pressure = Force/Area = [MLT^-2]/[L^2] = [ML^-1T^-2].

q: Which of the following is not a fundamental quantity in SI system?
exp: Force is a derived quantity obtained from mass and acceleration.

q: If velocity v depends on displacement x as v = kx^2, the dimension of k is:
The dimensional formula of gravitational constant G is:
Assertion (A): All scalar quantities have magnitude only. Reason (R): Scalars obey vector addition law.
opts: Both A and R are true and R explains A
Both A and R are true but R does not explain A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
exp: Scalars have only magnitude, but they do not follow vector addition law.

q: The SI unit of coefficient of viscosity is:
exp: Dynamic viscosity unit is pascal-second = Ns/m^2.

q: Which instrument is used to measure atmospheric pressure?
exp: Barometer is used to measure atmospheric pressure.

q: If force F, velocity v and power P are taken as fundamental quantities, the dimension of mass is:
exp: Since P = Fv, mass dimension becomes [FvP^-1].

q: A screw gauge has pitch 0.5 mm and 50 divisions on circular scale. Its least count is:
exp: Least count = pitch/number of divisions = 0.5/50 = 0.01 mm.

q: Which physical quantity has unit J s?
exp: Angular momentum has unit kg m^2 s^-1 = J s.

q: The error in measurement of radius is 2%. The percentage error in area of a circle is:
exp: Area ∝ r^2, so percentage error doubles: 2 × 2% = 4%.

q: Which of the following is a vector quantity?
exp: Momentum has both magnitude and direction.

q: The dimensions of Planck's constant are:
exp: Planck constant = energy × time = [ML^2T^-2][T].

q: One light year is a unit of:
exp: Light year measures the distance travelled by light in one year.

q: The dimensional formula of surface tension is:
exp: Surface tension = force/length = [MLT^-2]/[L] = [MT^-2].

q: A physical quantity is given by P = a + bt + ct^2. The dimensions of c are:
exp: Each term must have same dimensions, so c has dimensions [P]/[T^2].

q: Which of the following cannot be measured using vernier calipers?
exp: Mass is measured using a balance, not vernier calipers.

q: The dimensional formula of power is:
exp: Power = work/time = [ML^2T^-2]/[T].

q: Which quantity remains unchanged under unit transformation?
exp: Dimensions of a physical quantity remain invariant.

q: If the percentage errors in A and B are 2% and 3% respectively, then percentage error in A/B is:
exp: Errors add in division: 2% + 3% = 5%.

q: Which among the following has dimensions of frequency?
exp: All have dimension T^-1.

q: Assertion (A): Two quantities with same dimensions may represent different physical quantities. Reason (R): Work and torque have same dimensions.
opts: Both A and R are true and R explains A
Both A and R are true but R does not explain A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
exp: Torque and work both have dimensions [ML^2T^-2] but are different quantities.

q: The SI unit of magnetic flux is:
exp: Magnetic flux is measured in weber (Wb).

q: Which of the following has dimensions of momentum?
exp: All simplify to [MLT^-1], same as momentum.

q: The least count of a vernier caliper is 0.01 cm. Main scale reading is 2.3 cm and 6th vernier division coincides. Measurement is:
exp: Reading = MSR + (VC × LC) = 2.3 + 6×0.01 = 2.36 cm.

q: The dimensions of resistivity are:
exp: Resistivity = resistance × area/length.

q: Which quantity has dimensions independent of mass?
exp: Acceleration dimension is [LT^-2], independent of mass.

q: If force is proportional to velocity, F = kv, then dimensions of k are:
Which of the following is not a scalar?
exp: Electric field has direction, hence vector.

q: The dimensional formula of capacitance is:
exp: Capacitance = charge/potential difference.

q: A student measures length as 12.40 cm. The uncertain digit is:
exp: The last digit in a measured quantity is uncertain.

q: Which law can be verified using dimensional analysis?
exp: Dimensional homogeneity can verify equations like F = ma.

q: The dimensions of wavelength are:
exp: Wavelength is a length quantity.

q: Which of the following has dimensions of pressure?
exp: All have dimensions [ML^-1T^-2].

q: The SI unit of luminous intensity is:
exp: Candela is the SI base unit of luminous intensity.

q: The number of significant figures in 4.500 × 10^3 is:
exp: Trailing zeros after decimal are significant.

q: A quantity X is given by X = mv^2/r. The dimensions of X are:
exp: mv^2/r has dimensions [MLT^-2], same as force.

q: Which among the following is the most accurate instrument for small length measurement?
exp: Screw gauge has smaller least count than vernier caliper.

q: The dimensions of universal gas constant R are:
exp: R = PV/nT gives dimensions of energy per mole per kelvin.

q: If x = at^2 + bt + c, dimensions of a are:
exp: Since x has dimension L, a must have dimensions [LT^-2].

q: Which physical quantity has dimensions [M^0L^0T^0]?
exp: Relative density is a ratio, hence dimensionless.

q: Assertion (A): Dimensional analysis cannot determine numerical constants. Reason (R): Numerical constants are dimensionless.
opts: Both A and R are true and R explains A
Both A and R are true but R does not explain A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
exp: Dimensional analysis only compares dimensions and cannot predict pure numbers.

q: The SI unit of inductance is:
exp: Inductance is measured in henry.

q: Which quantity has dimensions [ML^2T^-3A^-1]?
exp: Potential difference = work/charge.

q: The percentage error in density when mass and volume errors are 2% and 3% respectively is:
exp: Density = m/V, so errors add: 2% + 3% = 5%.

q: Which of the following is correctly matched?
exp: Tesla is the SI unit of magnetic field.

q: A physical quantity depends on mass m, velocity v and radius r as X = mv/r. Dimensions of X are:
exp: mv/r = [MLT^-1]/L = [MT^-1]. Closest relevant interpretation involves frequency-like inverse time dependence.
